description: Application Interactive Services for OCCTSwift / OCCTSwiftViewport: selection-from-topology, translate/rotate manipulators, dimension annotations, and scene objects, pure Swift for Apple OSes.
rules: Import the package with `import OCCTSwiftAIS`; it transitively pulls OCCTSwift, OCCTSwiftViewport, and OCCTSwiftTools, so you usually `import OCCTSwift` and `import OCCTSwiftViewport` alongside it.

OCCTSwiftAIS is the interactive-services (AIS) layer atop the OCCT stack: OCCTSwift (B-Rep kernel) and OCCTSwiftViewport (Metal renderer) are kernels, OCCTSwiftTools is the Shape<->ViewportBody bridge, OCCTSwiftAIS adds selection/manipulators/dimensions.

`InteractiveContext(viewport: ViewportController())` is the per-scene state object (@MainActor, ObservableObject). Bind with `MetalViewportView(controller: ctx.viewport, bodies: $ctx.bodies)`; `ctx.display(shape)` returns an `InteractiveObject`.

Selection modes: `Set<SelectionMode>` of `.body`/`.face`/`.edge`/`.vertex` on `context.selectionMode` (changing clears it). `selection: Selection` exposes `.faces`/`.edges`/`.vertices`/`.bodies`; mutate via `select`/`deselect`/`clearSelection`.

`ManipulatorWidget(target:mode:)` is a translate or rotate gizmo (Mode.scale reserved). `install(in: context)`, drive with `hitTest(ndc:camera:aspect:)` + `beginDrag`/`updateDrag`/`endDrag` (NDC [-1,1], +Y up), observe `transform`; `uninstall()`.

For SwiftUI, `.attachManipulator(_:)` (Manipulator+SwiftUI.swift) wraps a viewport view with a high-priority drag gesture that drives the widget on a handle grab and orbits the camera otherwise; the widget must already be install(in:)-ed.

Dimensions conform to `Dimension`: `LinearDimension(from:to:plane:)`, `AngularDimension(arms:apex:)`, `RadialDimension(circularEdge:showDiameter:)`. Add with `context.add(_:)`, remove with `remove(_:)`; `refreshDimensionMeasurement(_:)` after moves.

`Trihedron`, `WorkPlane`, `Axis`, `PointCloudPresentation` build `ViewportBody`s via `makeBodies()`, each with `ownsBody(id:)`. Style via `PresentationStyle`/`HighlightStyle`. `InteractiveContext.remap(_:using:rebindingTo:strategy:)` remaps Selection.
